points: Roadways in India offer the advantage of lower construction costs and the ability to negotiate difficult terrains, serving as a vital feeder to other transport modes.
The road network is classified into six classes, including the Golden Quadrilateral Super Highways and strategic Border Roads that enhance accessibility in remote areas.
Railways are the principal mode for long-distance transport, integrating the national economy and accelerating the development of both industry and agriculture.
Pipelines have become a modern lifeline for transporting oil and gas, ensuring zero trans-shipment losses and minimal maintaining costs for inland refineries.
Waterways are the cheapest and most eco-friendly mode for bulk goods, with five major National Waterways and 12 major ports handling 95% of India's foreign trade.
Air travel provides the fastest and most comfortable connectivity, particularly essential for the difficult terrains of the North-eastern states and regional UDAN connectivity.
India's communication network is among the largest globally, featuring an extensive postal system and high-tech telecom integration through the Digital India initiative.
International trade acts as an economic barometer, where the balance of trade (difference between exports and imports) reflects the country's economic prosperity.
Tourism has emerged as a significant industry, promoting national integration and supporting local handicrafts while earning substantial foreign exchange.
